name: Ashur Bet Sargis
description:
'''Ashur Bet Sargis'''{{efn|Sometimes referred to as '''Ashur Sargis''', without the [[List of family name affixes|family name affix]] "''[[wikt:ܒܝܬ|bêṯ]]''".}} ({{langx|syr|ܐܫܘܪ ܒܝܬ ܣܪܓܝܤ}}; born July 2, 1949) is an [[Assyrian People|Assyrian]] singer, composer, guitarist and activist. He became famous in the [[Assyrian people|Assyrian]] communities worldwide for his nationalistic songs in the 1970s. His career peaked, and became prominent, in the 1990s with five albums released within that decade. 

His songs usually tend to be [[sentimental ballad]]s and [[anthem]]s, although he has made many [[folk dance]] music as well. To the contrast of his Assyrian peers in music, Sargis's music is [[easy listening|slow and mellow]]. He is also known for incorporating [[Music of Greece|Greek]] instruments such as the ''[[tambouras]]'' into his music.
